title: Viking Therapeutics Reports Fourth Quarter and Year-End 2024 Financial Results
text: Viking Therapeutics reported Q4 2024 net loss of $35.4 million ($0.32 per share) versus $24.6 million ($0.25 per share) year-over-year. For the full year 2024, the net loss reached $110.0 million ($1.01 per share) compared to $85.9 million ($0.91 per share) in 2023. The company ended 2024 with a robust cash position of $903 million in cash, equivalents, and short-term investments, up significantly from $362 million at year-end 2023. R&D expenses grew to $101.6 million for 2024 from $63.8 million in 2023, driven primarily by increased manufacturing costs, salaries, and stock-based compensation. General and administrative expenses rose to $49.3 million from $37.0 million, reflecting higher professional fees and personnel costs. CEO Brian Lian stated the company had an exciting and extremely productive year, highlighting positive data from four clinical trials. Phase 3 initiation for subcutaneous VK2735 in obesity is planned for Q2 2025, while the oral formulation continues evaluation in the VENTURE-Oral Dosing Phase 2 trial with results expected in H2 2025.
